# `@pagesmith/site` Full LLM Reference

`@pagesmith/site` is the Pagesmith site toolkit and the app-facing package for custom sites.

It owns:

- the `pagesmith-site` CLI (`pagesmith` remains a compatibility alias, and is the umbrella name for `pagesmith skills install`)
- preset loading
- server-side JSX (`@pagesmith/site/jsx-runtime`)
- CSS bundles (`@pagesmith/site/css/*`)
- browser runtime helpers (`@pagesmith/site/runtime/*`)
- Vite SSG helpers (`@pagesmith/site/vite`)
- shared SSG utilities (`@pagesmith/site/ssg-utils`)
- post-build validation (`@pagesmith/site/build-validator`)
- the shared bounded-concurrency worker pool re-exported from `@pagesmith/core` (`mapWithConcurrency`, `defaultConcurrency`), used by route pre-rendering

It builds on `@pagesmith/core` internally and re-exports the content-layer APIs that site consumers typically need.

Drop to `@pagesmith/core` directly only when the project intentionally wants the lower-level headless package instead of the site package.

Core still owns the underlying implementation for:

- collections
- schemas and loaders
- markdown rendering
- validation
- `pagesmithContent`

For site-first apps, keep those imports on `@pagesmith/site` so the project stays on one package surface.

Typical Vite wiring:

```ts
import { pagesmithContent, pagesmithSsg, sharedAssetsPlugin } from '@pagesmith/site/vite'
```

Typical framework-hosted app wiring:

```ts
import '@pagesmith/site/css/content'
import '@pagesmith/site/runtime/content'
```

In that shape, keep collection setup and markdown rendering on `@pagesmith/site` too, so the app stays on one Pagesmith package while still reusing the shipped prose/code-block presentation layer.

Typical JSX wiring:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "jsxImportSource": "@pagesmith/site"
  }
}
```

Preset rules:

- default preset: `@pagesmith/site/preset`
- override via `--preset`, `PAGESMITH_PRESET`, or config `preset` / `presets`
- use `pagesmith-docs` when the project wants the built-in docs workflow

Config helpers:

- `defineSiteConfig(config)` / `loadSiteConfig(configPath?)`
- `parseSiteConfig(config)` for schema-backed validation
- `normalizeBasePath`, `withBasePath`, `stripBasePath`, `formatPath`, `normalizeOrigin`
- `trailingSlash` config option (default: `false`) — slash-free URLs as default
- `formatPath(path, trailingSlash?)` replaces the legacy `withTrailingSlash()` utility
- `normalizeOrigin()` strips trailing slashes from origin URLs; origin is normalized at parse time via Zod transform
- `@pagesmith/site/schemas` exports `SiteUserConfigSchema`, `SitePageMeta` plus the related config helper schemas

Components and layouts:

- `HeroSection` — hero with badge, name, tagline, description, action buttons
- `ActionButtons` — primary/secondary button row
- `ContentMeta` — published date, updated date, draft badge, tags as pills
- `HomeLayout` — complete home page with hero + footer
- `ListingLayout` — wraps PageShell with SiteDocument
- `NotFoundLayout` — complete 404 page
- CSS classes: `.site-hero-*`, `.site-action-*`, `.site-pill`, `.site-tag-list`, `.site-content-meta`

SEO:

- `SitePageMeta` type with `ogType`, `publishedTime`, `modifiedTime`, `author`, `tags`, `articleType` (schema.org `@type` override, default `'Article'`)
- Twitter Card tags: `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:site`
- Article meta: `article:author`, `article:published_time`, `article:modified_time`, `article:tag`
- JSON-LD: `SiteDocument` emits schema.org structured data unless `seo.jsonLd === false` (default `true`) -- an `Article`/`BlogPosting`/etc. block when `meta.ogType === 'article'`, or a `WebSite` block when `isHome` is passed. Values come from data already resolved for OG/meta tags (headline, description, dates, author, canonical URL, absolute image). Also exported standalone: `buildArticleStructuredData(input)`, `buildWebsiteStructuredData(input)`, `serializeJsonLd(data)` (escapes `<` so the JSON safely embeds in an inline `<script type="application/ld+json">`).
- `SiteFooter` accepts an `extraContent` slot

SSG utilities (`@pagesmith/site/ssg-utils`):

- `generateFeed(entries, config)` -- RSS 2.0 feed. Entries: `title`, `path`, optional `publishedDate` (excluded from the feed when absent), `description` (falls back to `title`), `tags` (-> `<category>`). Config: `origin`, `basePath`, `title`, `description`, `language`, `limit` (default 50, newest-first by `publishedDate`), `buildDate` (defaults to now; pass an explicit `Date` for deterministic tests).
- `generateSitemap(routes, config)` -- `urlset` sitemap.xml. Routes are paths relative to the base URL; use `''` or `'/'` for home. Config: `origin`, `basePath`. `@pagesmith/docs` delegates its own sitemap generation to this same function; output is unchanged from its previous in-package implementation.
- `runPagefindIndexing(outDir, options?)` -- search index generation after build.

Build validation (`@pagesmith/site/build-validator`):

- `validateBuildOutput(options)` / `runBuildValidation(options)` -- structural checks over emitted HTML (internal links, image structure, theme variants, trailing-slash consistency, etc.)
- `checkSitemap` (default `false`) -- cross-checks `sitemap.xml` against emitted HTML: every `<loc>` must resolve to a file (error) and every indexable non-redirect HTML page (excluding `404.html`) should appear in the sitemap (warning). No-op when `sitemap.xml` is absent.
- `checkBundledAssets` (default `false`) -- verifies the entry `index.html` references at least one resolvable bundled CSS asset and one bundled JS asset (catches empty/broken production bundles).

pagesmithSsg `beforeBuild` hook:

- `pagesmithSsg({ beforeBuild })` accepts an optional `SsgBeforeBuildHook`: `(ctx) => void | Promise<void>` where `ctx` is `{ rootDir, outDir, config, logger }` (`config` is Vite's resolved config; `logger` is prefixed `pagesmith:ssg`). Runs once in `closeBundle`, after the client build's assets already exist in `outDir` but before route pre-rendering -- a safe place to write additional static files (feeds, sitemaps, generated content) that the route renderer will not overwrite. A thrown error aborts the build with `pagesmithSsg beforeBuild hook failed: <message>`. The runner is separately exported as `runBeforeBuildHook(hook, ctx)` for unit testing.

Skills install (`pagesmith skills install`, umbrella CLI shipped from this package):

- Auto-detects which of `@pagesmith/core` / `@pagesmith/site` / `@pagesmith/docs` are installed and writes, for every skill each ships, a canonical pointer stub at `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (links back to `node_modules/@pagesmith/<pkg>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, carries an HTML-comment version marker) plus thin mirror stubs under detected/requested harness dirs (`.claude/skills/`, `.cursor/skills/`, `.codex/skills/`, `.continue/skills/`).
- Flags: `--package <pkg>` (repeatable), `--dir <path>`, `--harness <list>`, `--only <name>` (repeatable), `--check` (verify-only; nonzero exit on missing/stale/orphaned stubs -- wire into CI), `--dry-run`, `--json`.
- `pagesmith-core skills` is a deprecated alias that forwards to the identical installer and prints a one-line deprecation note to stderr; it will be removed in a future minor.

Theme-aware content:

The shipped CSS bundles include utility classes for color-scheme-responsive content:
- `.ps-figure` / `.ps-figure-themed` / `.ps-figure-zoomable` on pipeline-generated figure elements
- Markdown images are auto-wrapped in `<figure class="ps-figure ps-figure-zoomable">` with `<picture>` for raster formats
- Consecutive `-light`/`-dark` image pairs are auto-merged into `<figure class="ps-figure ps-figure-themed ps-figure-zoomable">`
- `.only-light` / `.only-dark` on `<img>` for manual light/dark image pairs
- `.show-on-light` / `.show-on-dark` for toggling any element by color scheme
- `.invert-on-dark` for inverting a single image in dark mode
- These respond to the `color-scheme-auto|light|dark` class on `<html>` (default: auto)
- `initThemedImages()` in chrome runtime provides JS-enhanced theme switching with MutationObserver on `<html>` class, WeakMap-based source caching, and proper cleanup

Image-zoom (JS-only progressive enhancement):

- Every figure carries a hidden `<button class="ps-img-zoom-btn" hidden data-ps-img-zoom-btn>`. Without JS the button stays hidden via the `[hidden]` attribute.
- `initImageZoom()` (in `@pagesmith/site/runtime/image-zoom`, also bundled into `runtime/content` and `runtime/standalone`) unhides every button on init and opens a singleton full-viewport `<div class="ps-img-zoom-modal" role="dialog" aria-modal="true">` on click.
- The modal sizes the image to fit the constrained viewport axis at 100% zoom (preserves aspect ratio). Toolbar `+` / `-` step by 10%; clamps are 50% min, 400% max for raster, 1000% max for SVG. `Ctrl/Cmd+wheel` zooms; plain wheel pans (default scroll). `Esc` / close button shuts the modal and restores focus to the trigger button.
- The runtime reads `data-zoom-src` (or themed `data-zoom-src-light` / `data-zoom-src-dark` with matching `data-zoom-type-*`) from the image and swaps source when `<html>` toggles `color-scheme-light` / `color-scheme-dark` (MutationObserver, attached only while the modal is open).
- CSS lives in `@pagesmith/site/styles/content/image-zoom.css` — bundled into both `content.css` and `standalone.css`.

- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/skills/pagesmith-site-setup/references/setup-site.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/README.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/REFERENCE.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/skills/pagesmith-site-setup/references/site-guidelines.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/skills/pagesmith-site-setup/references/usage.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/core/REFERENCE.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/docs/REFERENCE.md`
